title: Groodo
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Corporate |image= |name=Groodo |homeworld=[[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031106hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt! #5}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Let's get down to business ... Senator, I promised you a fortune in return for your help in putting Fondor out of the starship-manufacturing business. Now, I followed your instructions to the letter, constructing the fake ''Sun Runner'' to distract your people as well as the Nallastians, supplying the weapons to wipe out Fondor's starship yards, and so on. You were supposed to prevent any Jedi from meddling. What went wrong?"
"The Jedi were more cunning than I realized. However, they are presently on Fondor's moon, Nallastia. If we are to fulfill our plan to bring Fondor to ruin, we must act now.|Groodo the Hutt, manufacturer of the ''Sun Runner'' replica, and Senator Rodd of Fondor|''[[Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess]]''}} '''Groodo''' the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]—the brother of [[Rigorra]] and the father of [[Boonda/Legends|Boonda]]—operated what he claimed to be 'legitimate' business interests, particularly in [[Starship/Legends|starship]] manufacturing, during the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|I'm the one who had you brought here."
"What? ... Excuse me, but just who in the flizzards are you to have had us abducted? ... Not that I'm angry with you, mister ... what's your name again?"
"Count Dooku of Serenno."
"You're a Jedi?"
"A former Jedi."
"Really? They let you keep your lightsaber though, huh?"
"They never really had any say in the matter.|Darth Tyranus and Groodo, as the Sith Lord recruits the "Fondor trio" to his cause on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]|''[[Star Wars Adventures 6: The Warlords of Balmorra]]''}} The [[Crime lord/Legends|crimelord's]] luxurious domicile was a five-level [[palace]] located a kilometer east of [[Calamar]]—the [[Capital/Legends|capital city]] of [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] in the [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]] and a major center of [[hypernautics]] and advanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] specialization. The Hutt's huge [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] compound included a [[Factory/Legends|factory]] and was surrounded by a high wall.''[[Episode I Adventures 1: Search for the Lost Jedi]]''''[[Star Wars Adventures 4: Jango Fett vs. the Razor Eaters]]'' Groodo's criminal activities were fronted by 'legitimate' business interests in Esseles, including the operation of his successful compound factory that specialized in the manufacture of customized hyperdrive [[Engine/Legends|engines]].''[[Episode I Adventures 2: The Bartokk Assassins]]''''[[Episode I Adventures 3: The Fury of Darth Maul]]'' While still maintaining ownership of the factory, he later turned it over to his son Boonda when he focused his energies on the development of '''Groodo Starship Yards''', his modular industrial [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] that orbited Esseles.''[[Star Wars Adventures 1: Hunt the Sun Runner]]'' The starship yard had the unusual feature of a mammoth ''enclosed'' [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. The practicality of [[Shipyard/Legends|orbital yards]] was that they allowed for the construction of large starships in zero [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]. But since ''enclosed'' hangars restricted movement around the ships during construction, their only good use was to maintain ''secrecy''. Groodo's hangar was large enough to conceal a 400-meter-long starship: it was, of course, in due time, the construction site of the ill-fated, full-size [[False Sun Runner|replica]] of the legendary ''[[Sun Runner]]''. Responsible for the [[Bartokk]] attack on [[Trinkatta Starships]]' factory on [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], Groodo had been hired by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] to build a small but powerful [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] eradicated Groodo's plans to steal from Trinkatta both the hyperdrive prototype and the 50 [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|''Vulture''-class droid starfighters]] its manufacture would power. But instead of killing him, Maul left Groodo to live on in fear. In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], Groodo at last was able to produce a counterfeit of the ancient transport vessel, the ''Sun Runner'', which he intended to use to destroy the [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|Fondor Shipyards]] in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]], in order to gain more business for his own factories.''[[Star Wars Adventures 2: The Cavern of Screaming Skulls]]'' The replica ship was deliberately distressed to make it look four thousand years old, but outfitted with a state-of-the-art [[Hypermatter reactor/Legends|power source]], [[Navigational sensor|navigational]] [[Sensor jammer/Legends|equipment]], and [[Guided missile/Legends|armaments]]. Groodo's scheme, carried out in collusion with [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Rodd]] and [[Hurlo Holowan]], to both destroy the shipyards and take over and destroy the [[Fondor Spaceport]] and surface factories was foiled, however, by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]]. Groodo enlisted the help of his brother, the Balmorra warlord Rigorra, to create false evidence, using all the necessary resources and technology, that would blame Groodo's own son, the relatively innocent Boonda, for concocting the entire scheme to wreak havoc on the starship yards of Fondor. Soon thereafter, however, Groodo was hunted down by both [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who was hired by [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]] to take the Hutt alive, and hunters [[Cradossk/Legends|Cradossk]] and his son [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], who were simultaneously hired for the job (but to bring in his head) by [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]], [[Foreman/Legends|Foreman]] of the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] and a member of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]]. Fett, in the end, was the one to achieve the task at Rigorra's castle on [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]], and he turned Groodo over to Darth Tyranus alive. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], impressed with the concept of the ''Sun Runner'' scheme hatched by Groodo, Senator Rodd and Holowan—and with the ingenuity, ambition, talent, and secrecy they demonstrated—wished to recruit them to [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|his cause]].''[[Star Wars Adventures 5: The Shape-Shifter Strikes]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Are you going to kill us?"
"Kill you? That would be such a waste of talent! You see, I'm working on a project, something that may take years. I can't tell you all about it, but as you may have already assumed, Fondor is an important part of my plans."
"So ... you're not going to kill us?"
"On the contrary, Senator. I'm recruiting you for my cause. Although your scheme for Fondor failed, it showed a great deal of ambition and ingenuity, and it's also to your credit that neither the Fondor Space Patrol nor the Jedi Knights ever discovered your plot. I admire people who can keep secrets."
"You're hiring us then?"
"That's right."
"I don't get it. What if we don't want to work for you?"
"Then you won't leave this room alive."
"Ah. I get it."
"I knew you would. Now, let us adjourn to more pleasant surroundings. We have much to discuss.|Holowan, Rodd, and Groodo field questions to Darth Tyranus, who recruits them to his cause|}} Groodo, a very old and "incredibly corpulent" Hutt, spared few luxuries—in life, in general, but also on his private [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. Like most Hutts, however, his aesthetic tastes were questionable. Groodo's cruiser had broad decorative fins, large oval [[viewport]]s, and a lurid color scheme of red, orange, and yellow; Groodo's lush cabin was lined with striped animal pelts. Everything was designed for maximum visibility and to show off Groodo's considerable wealth. Though Groodo indulged in many pleasures, revenge was at the top of his list: the Hutt actually looked forward to being wronged, so that he might exact vengeance upon his enemies. Groodo's palatial compound on Esseles was defended by a squadron of [[Razor Eater]]s, vicious assassin droids designed by Hurlo Holowan; their razor-sharp teeth could bite through virtually anything. Jango Fett was forced to contend with Groodo's Razor Eaters when on his Darth Tyranus-commissioned search of the Hutt and his accomplices, Rodd and Holowan. Groodo enjoyed eating handfuls of crawling [[mukkmaggot]]s. Beyond being a favorite food, they helped to steady his nerves. Having been made a warlord of Balmorra by his brother, Groodo also indulged himself in Rigorra's fermented-beverage cellar, to which Groodo was given a key. ==Appearances== *''[[Episode I Adventures 3: The Fury of Darth Maul]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Episode I Adventures 4: Jedi Emergency]]'' *''[[Star Wars Adventures 1: Hunt the Sun Runner]]'' *''[[Star Wars Adventures 2: The Cavern of Screaming Skulls]]'' {{Hologram}} *''[[Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess]]'' *''[[Star Wars Adventures 4: Jango Fett vs. the Razor Eaters]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Adventures 5: The Shape-Shifter Strikes]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Adventures 6: The Warlords of Balmorra]]'' *[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] {{Ab|Millennium Falcon (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031016hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt! #4}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031106hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt! #5}} *{{FactFile|140|HUT 15-18|The Hutts}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Businesspeople]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Esselians]] [[Category:Hutts]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Trade Federation members]]
